question: If an object is described as 'solid', it means ________.
choices: it is empty on the inside
it is curved and has no flat surfaces
it is completely hard and not hollow
it is covered in geometric patterns

question: If an object is described as 'spherical', its shape is ________.
choices: flat and perfectly even on all sides
long and pointed at both ends
square with equal sides and angles
round like a ball or sphere

question: The phrase 'take something into account' means to ________.
choices: consider or remember something when judging a situation
record facts and figures in an official document
reject an idea because it is not relevant
explain the details of a plan to a group of people

question: The adjective 'enormous' means ________.
choices: very small and difficult to see without help
extremely large in size
perfectly round in shape
very old and belonging to a past era

question: If an object is described as 'circular', it ________.
choices: has many flat surfaces joined at sharp angles
is shaped like a perfect cone
is made of soft material that can be bent easily
is round and shaped like a circle

question: Choose the correct preposition: 'She did it _____ purpose; she planned it from the very beginning.'
choices: at
in
by
on

question: Which sentence uses 'in addition' correctly?
choices: In addition, she left the cinema before the film ended
In addition, everyone arrived to the concert late without warning
In addition to the main role, she also played a small supporting part
She was in addition surprised by the unexpected ending of the film

question: Choose the correct meaning of 'on their part' in context: 'It requires less effort on their part.'
choices: it is part of their regular routine
it is a responsibility they share with others
it is something their parents ask them to do
it is done by them / it is their effort

question: 'It's quite likely that the map wasn't an original copy.' Choose the correct past modal form.
choices: The map must have been an original copy
The map can't have been an original copy
The map might not have been an original copy
The map would have been an original copy

question: 'I'm sure they were not perfectly geometric.' Choose the correct past modal form.
choices: They might have been perfectly geometric
They must have been perfectly geometric
They would have been perfectly geometric
They can't have been perfectly geometric

question: 'It is possible that it was a king's helmet.' Choose the correct past modal form.
choices: It might have been a king's helmet
It must have been a king's helmet
It can't have been a king's helmet
It would have been a king's helmet

question: 'The helmet was most likely the most important part of his equipment.' Choose the correct past modal form.
choices: The helmet can't have been important
The helmet must have been the most important part
The helmet might not have been very important
The helmet wouldn't have been important

question: Choose the correctly reduced form of: 'Because he has won several awards, the actor's work is well-known.'
choices: Because winning several awards, the actor's work is well-known
The actor's work is well-known, winning several awards
Having won several awards, the actor's work is well-known
Won several awards, the actor's work is well-known

question: Choose the correctly reduced form of: 'As they are best friends, they enjoy working together.'
choices: Having been best friends, they enjoy working together
They are best friends, enjoying working together
Best friends, they enjoy working together
Being best friends, they enjoy working together

question: Choose the correctly reduced form of: 'He lost my key. He couldn't get into the house.'
choices: Not having lost my key, he couldn't get into the house
He lost my key, not getting into the house
Losing my key, he couldn't get into the house
Having lost my key, he couldn't get into the house

question: Cameron starred in the film although he found it difficult to read. Choose the correctly reduced form.
choices: Cameron starred in the film despite finding it difficult to read
Cameron starred in the film, not finding it difficult to read
Finding it difficult to read, Cameron didn't star in the film
Despite not finding it difficult to read, Cameron starred in the film

question: Which expression is used to ASK FOR CLARIFICATION during informal negotiation?
choices: I could go along with that
How would you feel about trying a different approach?
So, are you saying that we should change the whole plan?
I don't know if I would fancy that

question: Which expression is used to show you are NOT accepting a suggestion in informal negotiation?
choices: Fair enough
I'm happy with that
That sounds like a good idea
I don't know if I would fancy that

question: The phrase 'Supposing we...' is used in informal negotiation to ________.
choices: ask for clarification about a previous point
reject a suggestion politely and professionally
make a suggestion about what could be done
make a final decision after discussing all options

question: The phrase 'So how can we resolve this?' is used to ________.
choices: ask for clarification about a suggestion already made
accept a suggestion with complete enthusiasm
reject a suggestion and propose a better alternative
move towards making a final decision in negotiation

question: 'The ________ in his performance was amazing — it made the whole audience burst out laughing.' What is the missing performance vocabulary word?
choices: cast
season
punchline
set

question: 'We missed yesterday's episode, so we need to ________ tonight.' Choose the correct phrase.
choices: deliver the punchline
heckle the cast
play the lead role
catch up on the latest episode

question: The adjective 'vibrant' describes colours or performances that are ________.
choices: dull and lacking in energy or interest
unpleasant and difficult to look at directly
very quiet and unlikely to attract attention
full of life, energy, and bright colour

question: The adjective 'rich' used to describe decorative designs means ________.
choices: having a lot of money invested in its creation
using only the most expensive materials available
deep, full, and impressive in quality or appearance
belonging to a royal family or wealthy background

question: The adjective 'subtle' used in the context of art means ________.
choices: extremely loud and immediately noticeable from far away
skillfully done in a way that is not immediately obvious
using very few colours to create a simple design
representing a traditional cultural event or ceremony

question: 'The acting was so unconvincing that ________.'
choices: the audience could not stop applauding enthusiastically
critics praised it as one of the best performances of the year
everyone agreed it was the most exciting show they had seen
nobody could believe in any of the characters at all

question: 'She plays the role with a ________ innocence about it.' (positive quality typical of a child)
choices: worthless
childish
childlike
classic

question: 'The director's reaction to criticism was extremely ________.' (disapproval of behaviour that makes one seem like a child)
choices: historic
tasteful
alive
childish

question: Read the following excerpt: 'A more recent study found that spoilers did negatively affect people's enjoyment. 412 university students were given several short stories.' According to this text, how many university students participated in the study?
Read the following excerpt: 'A more recent study found that spoilers did negatively affect people's enjoyment.' What did the study conclude about stories that had been spoiled?
choices: They were enjoyed more because readers knew what to expect
They were considered to be less thought-provoking
They were rated higher in quality than unspoiled stories
They were not finished by most of the participants

question: Read the following excerpt: 'We enjoy it more the second time because we are free to notice more of the detail.' What reason does the author give for re-reading a beloved book?
choices: To understand the ending more clearly the second time
To share a summary of the story with friends
To enjoy noticing more detail because we are free from suspense
To discover hidden meanings the author included deliberately

question: According to information about Jordan's parliament, the 80 members of the House of Deputies are ________.
choices: appointed by the King for six-year terms
chosen by the Senate from a list of qualified candidates
given their positions by the Prime Minister
elected by the people every four years

question: According to information about Jordan's parliament, Senators must ________.
choices: be under the age of 40 and have a university degree
be elected by the general public in a national election
be over 40 years of age and have worked in important government positions
be chosen by the House of Deputies after a public vote

question: According to information about Jordan's National Assembly, it consists of two houses. The upper house is ________.
choices: the House of Deputies (Majlis Al-Nuwaab)
the House of Representatives
the Council of Ministers
the Senate (Majlis Al-Aayan)

question: According to the article on resilience, when you cannot improve a difficult situation, you should ________.
choices: give up and accept that things will never change
blame others and express your frustration openly
avoid thinking about the situation completely
always learn from it and what you could do differently

question: According to the article on resilience, the support of others ________.
choices: is unnecessary if you are naturally a resilient person
can make all the difference between being resilient and feeling overwhelmed
is only useful when provided by professional counsellors
should only be used as a last resort when all else has failed

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', the phrase 'the darkest evening of the year' refers to ________.
choices: a stormy night in January during a power cut
the evening before an important journey begins
the Winter Solstice, which is the longest night of the year
a night in February when the snow is deepest

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', the line 'But I have promises to keep' suggests that the speaker ________.
choices: has forgotten the directions to his destination
is worried he will be seen stopping on someone else's land
is afraid of the dark and does not want to continue alone
has responsibilities that prevent him from staying any longer

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', how many syllables does each line of the poem contain?
In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', what does the horse shaking its harness bells most likely suggest?
choices: It is celebrating the beauty of the snowy forest
It is asking whether stopping here is a mistake
It recognises the owner of the woods passing by
It is trying to attract the attention of people in the village
